title: Zeolitic imidazolate framework materials, their synthesis and use
abstract: A novel zeolitic imidazolate framework material comprises a partially saturated benzimidazole or a partially saturated substituted benzimidazole as a linking ligand, optionally together with unsaturated benzimidazole or an unsaturated substituted benzimidazole as a further linking ligand.
claims:
Claims 1. A zeolitic imidazolate framework material comprising a partially saturated benzimidazole or a partially saturated substituted benzimidazole as a linking ligand. 2. The framework material of claim 1, wherein the partially saturated benzimidazole comprises 4,5,6,7-tetrahydrobenzimidazole. 3. The framework material of claim 1, wherein the partially saturated substituted benzimidazole comprises 4,5,6,7-tetrahydro-3H-imidazo[4,5-c]pyridine. 4. The framework material of any preceding claim, further comprising an unsaturated benzimidazole or an unsaturated substituted benzimidazole as a linking ligand. 5. The framework material of any preceding claim, further comprising one or more divalent transition metal ions. 6. The framework material of any one of claims 1 to 4, further comprising one or more zinc ions or one or more cobalt ions. 7. The framework material of any one of claims 1 to 4, further comprising a 1 : 1 mixture of lithium and boron ions. 8. A zeolitic imidazolate framework material comprising zinc and 4,5,6,7- tetrahy drob enzimi dazol e . 9. A zeolitic imidazolate framework material comprising zinc and 4,5,6,7-tetrahydro-3H- imidazo[4,5-c]pyridine. 10. The framework material of claim 8 or 9, further comprising benzimidazole or 5- azab enzimi dazol e . 11. The framework material of claim 10 further comprising from 0 mol% to 99 mol% of benzimidazole or 5-azabenzimidazole, based on the total amount of benzimidazole or 5- azabenzimidazole and 4,5,6,7-tetrahydrobenzimidazole or 4,5,6,7-tetrahydro-3H-imidazo[4,5- cjpyridine in the material. 12. The framework material of any preceding claim, wherein said framework material exhibits the SOD framework type. 13. The framework material of any proceeding claim, wherein said framework material exhibits the RHO framework type. 14. A method of adsorbing an element or compound from a fluid, the method comprising the step of contacting the fluid with the zeolitic imidazolate framework material of any one of claims 1 to 14. 15. The process of claim 14, wherein the element or compound comprises hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen, a noble gas, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide, 

 hydrogen sulfide, ammonia, methane, higher carbon number hydrocarbons, an alcohol, an amine or a mixture thereof. 16. A method of making a zeolitic imidazolate framework material comprising the step of reacting a mixture of a source of a partially saturated benzimidazole or a source of a partially saturated substituted benzimidazole with a source of zinc in the presence of a solvent at a temperature sufficient to form the zeolitic imidazolate framework material. 17. The method of claim 16 in which the temperature is at least 20 Â°C. 18. The method of claim 16 or claim 17 in which the partially saturated benzimidazole comprises 4,5,6,7-tetrahydrobenzimidazole. 19. The method of claim 16 or claim 17 in which the partially saturated substituted benzimidazole comprises 4,5,6,7-tetrahydro-3H-imidazo[4,5-c]pyridine. 20. The method of any one of claims 16 to 19, in which the source of zinc comprises zinc acetate. 21. The method of any one of claims 16 to 20, wherein the mixture further comprises an unsaturated benzimidazole or an unsaturated substituted benzimidazole. 22. A zeolitic imidazolate framework material made by the method of any one of claims 16 to 21, wherein the framework material exhibits the SOD framework type. 23. A zeolitic imidazolate framework material made by the method of any one of claims 16 to 21, wherein the framework material exhibits the RHO framework type.
